previous_picks.rationale: Valero has surged +86% in 1 year and +63.74% YTD, with a 1-month gain of +2.41% and the highest short interest (4.31%) signalling strong momentum. A P/E of 19.57 is moderate for the refining sector, and recent news suggests it may be 34% undervalued ahead of an earnings beat.

Marathon Petroleum boasts a +51% 1-year return and +62.71% YTD, with a 1-week surge of +5.04% and positive news sentiment (0.183). Its P/E of 17.55 supports value, and it is well-positioned in a softer oil-price environment.

EOG Resources has a strong YTD of +24.03% and a low P/E of 12.86, with a 312 basis point dividend yield. News sentiment is positive at 0.289, and Morgan Stanley maintains equal weight, reflecting steady fundamentals.

Equinor leads the oil majors with a +33.60% YTD return and a 1-week gain of +1.52%, while carrying a low P/E of 14.5 and a high dividend yield of 4.74%. Recent news of a $1B contract win with Transocean and very positive news sentiment (0.285) support upside.

TotalEnergies has a solid +27% 1-year return and +16.55% YTD, with a very low P/E of 11.38 and the highest dividend yield in the group (5.5%). Recent acquisition in Abu Dhabi gas and methane initiatives add catalysts despite slightly negative sentiment.

NextEra Energy shows steady momentum with a 1-month gain of +5.21% and a YTD of +10.71%, supported by a low short interest near zero. Its P/E of 22.42 is reasonable for a utility with AI data center power push and dividend plans, as highlighted by recent news.

SSE has a +35% 1-year return and +12.37% YTD, with a 1-month gain of +7.32% and a 1-week gain of +3.55%. Its dividend yield of 2.74% and positive news sentiment (0.149) support a buy, though a fair value edge lower tempers confidence.

Shell has a low P/E of 12.15, a comfortable dividend yield of 4.01%, and a 1-week gain of +0.89%. Recent $3B U.S. asset sale aligns with a $3B buyback, supporting shareholder returns, though near-term LNG volumes face headwinds.

ExxonMobil has a +23% 1-year return and +13.28% YTD, with a moderate P/E of 23.08 and a very low beta of 0.162. Q2 adjusted net income is estimated at $15.9B, implying a 2.8% quarterly profit yield, and the Texas redomiciliation is a minor catalyst.

Chevron has a +15% 1-year return and +10.60% YTD, with a 1-day gain of +2.05% and a dividend yield of 4.21%. Q2 adjusted net income is estimated at $9.9B (2.9% profit yield), and Project Kilby for data center power adds growth optionality.
